title: Cloud Thoughts
content:
Above the clouds I sail, above the clouds,
         And wish my mind
    Above its clouds could climb as well,
         And leave behind
    The world and all its crowds,
         And ever dwell
    In such a calm and limpid solitude
    With ne'er a breath unkind or harsh or rude
         To break the spell - 
    With ne'er a thought to drive away
    The golden splendour of the day.
    Alone and lost beneath the tranquil blue,
         My God! With you!

     Written in an Aeroplane.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Bewsher, titled "Cloud Thoughts", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
